title: 🛣️ AetherMoore/EDE Realistic Roadmap
content:
# 🛣️ AetherMoore/EDE Realistic Roadmap

Status: Engineering-Focused Development Plan
Timeline: 18-24 months to production-ready
Budget: $50,000-100,000 (assuming partnerships)
Core Principle: Build What's Provable
No Overclaims. No Hype. Just Engineering.
Phase 1: Security Validation (Months 1-6)
Goal
Answer the question: "Is this actually secure?"
Milestones
Month 1-2: Cryptographer Partnership
Post RFP to university cryptography labs
Interview 3-5 PhD cryptographers
Select partner (budget: $10k-15k consultation)
Sign NDA and collaboration agreement
Month 3-4: Formal Security Model
Develop threat model (adversary capabilities, attack surfaces)
Define security properties (IND-CPA, IND-CCA2, etc.)
Attempt formal reduction to hard problem (LWE, SVP, or equivalent)
Document what's provable vs. heuristic
Month 5-6: Real-World Attack Testing
Hire red team for penetration testing (budget: $5k-10k)
Test against:
Document vulnerabilities and fixes
Deliverables
✅ Formal security proof (or honest assessment of limitations)
✅ Red team report with attack vectors
✅ Decision: Continue, pivot, or abandon
Budget: $15,000-25,000
Phase 2: Quantum Hardware Validation (Months 7-12)
Goal
Prove (or disprove) quantum resistance on real hardware.
Milestones
Month 7-8: Qiskit Circuit Implementation
Rewrite expansion algorithm as quantum circuits
Implement Grover's attack on AetherMoore keyspace
Implement Shor's attack on base crypto layer
Add noise models (depolarizing, thermal, etc.)
Month 9-10: IBM Quantum Testing
Apply for IBM Quantum Network membership (academic tier: free)
Run circuits on real hardware:
Measure:
Month 11-12: Error Correction Analysis
Implement Surface Code error correction
Calculate overhead: physical qubits per logical qubit
Determine if quantum resistance is achievable with current NISQ hardware
Document honest findings
Decision Point
If quantum resistance is validated: Continue to Phase 3
If not: Pivot to classical distributed protocol (still valuable)
Deliverables
✅ Qiskit implementation with real hardware results
✅ Honest assessment: "Quantum-resistant" or "Classical-only"
✅ Published results (arXiv preprint or GitHub)
Budget: $5,000-10,000 (hardware time + researcher time)
Phase 3: Production Engineering (Months 13-18)
Goal
Build a deployable system (even if not quantum-resistant).
Milestones
Month 13-14: Architecture Redesign
Remove unproven features (if validation failed)
Add CRYSTALS-Kyber/Dilithium wrappers
Implement proper error correction (FEC for space links)
Cap bandwidth expansion (2× max, not exponential)
Month 15-16: Production Implementation
AWS Lambda production deployment
Add monitoring (CloudWatch, X-Ray)
Implement constant-time operations (side-channel resistance)
Add comprehensive logging and alerting
Month 17-18: Integration Testing
Test in Spiralverse context (if applicable)
Test with AI agent authentication use case
Load testing and performance optimization
Security audit by independent firm (budget: $10k-25k)
Deliverables
✅ Production-ready implementation
✅ Security audit report
✅ Documentation and API specs
✅ Integration examples
Budget: $20,000-40,000
Phase 4: Publication & Patent (Months 19-24)
Goal
Share findings honestly and protect what's actually novel.
Milestones
Month 19-20: Academic Publication
Write paper documenting:
Submit to conference (e.g., ACM CCS, USENIX Security, IEEE S&P)
Or publish on arXiv if academic venue not achieved
Month 21-22: Patent Preparation
Work with patent attorney to identify what's actually novel:
File provisional patent ($300 USPTO + $3k-8k attorney)
File full patent within 12 months if validation supports it
Month 23-24: Open Source Release
Publish implementation on GitHub (MIT or Apache 2.0 license)
Create documentation site
Write blog post with honest findings
Engage with community
Deliverables
✅ Peer-reviewed publication (or arXiv preprint)
✅ Patent filing (if warranted)
✅ Open source release
✅ Honest case study
Budget: $5,000-15,000
Total Budget & Timeline
Costs
Phase 1: $15,000-25,000 (Security validation)
Phase 2: $5,000-10,000 (Quantum testing)
Phase 3: $20,000-40,000 (Production engineering)
Phase 4: $5,000-15,000 (Publication & patent)
Total: $45,000-90,000
Timeline
Fast track: 18 months (with full-time focus)
Realistic: 24 months (part-time, with partnerships)
Conservative: 30 months (if pivots required)
Alternative: Lean Approach
If Budget is Limited ($5,000-10,000)
Focus on:
Academic partnership (often free for co-authorship)
IBM Quantum Network (free academic tier)
Open source tools (Qiskit, QuTip, all free)
Self-publish on arXiv (free)
Defer patent until validation complete
Skip:
Paid red team testing (use bug bounty instead)
Paid security audit (get peer review via publication)
Patent attorney (file provisional yourself)
Timeline: 24-30 months
Budget: $5,000-10,000
What Success Looks Like
Best Case
Formal security proof achieved
Quantum resistance validated on real hardware
Peer-reviewed publication accepted
Patent granted
Production deployment successful
Community adoption
Realistic Case
Security model documented (even if limitations found)
Quantum resistance disproven, pivot to classical protocol
arXiv preprint published
Patent filed for engineering innovations
Solid production system for AI agent auth
Honest case study that builds credibility
Worst Case
Fatal flaws discovered
Quantum claims abandoned
Project repositioned as research learning
Open source release as educational tool
Still valuable: Learned what doesn't work, helped community
Key Principles
1. Honesty First
Document failures as well as successes
No overclaims in marketing or pitches
Build reputation on transparency
2. Iterate Based on Evidence
If quantum resistance fails, pivot
If conlang layer is weak, remove it
Follow the data, not the ego
3. Partner with Experts
Don't pretend to be a cryptographer if you're not
Bring in PhDs for validation
Co-author publications for credibility
4. Open Source What's Learned
Even negative results help the community
Build reputation through contribution
Let others validate (or invalidate) your work
Next Steps (This Week)
[ ] Review and accept this realistic roadmap
[ ] Update all AetherMoore/EDE documentation with honest limitations
[ ] Draft email to university cryptography labs (partnership inquiry)
[ ] Create GitHub repo: aethermoore-research (not quantum-proof-crypto)
[ ] Write honest blog post: "What we're learning about AI agent authentication"
Related Pages
🔐 AetherMoore Protocol – Harmonic Conlang Cipher System - Updated technical specs
⚠️ Vulnerability Assessment - Security analysis
💎 Aethermore Protocol - Investor Pitch Deck - Integration context
Created: January 9, 2026
Philosophy: Build what's provable. Document what's learned. Never oversell.
Status: Ready for execution
